card.intuition: Track which elements belong to the same group by linking them into trees — find the root to identify the group, union to merge two groups.
card.analogy: Social circles: each person knows their group leader. To check if two people share a circle, trace both to their leaders. To merge two circles, make one leader report to the other.
card.time_complexity: O(α(n)) per operation (inverse Ackermann — effectively O(1))
when_to_use: Detecting cycles in an undirected graph
gotchas: Without path compression and union by rank, each operation degrades to O(n) — always use both optimizations
Union-Find does not support split/disconnect operations — it's union-only
For directed graphs, connected components require Tarjan's or Kosaraju's, not Union-Find

exercise.prompt: Complete the Union-Find with path compression and union by rank.
exercise.solution:
self.parent[x] = self.find(self.parent[x])  # path compression
self.rank[px] += 1  # increase rank only when both had same rank
hints: Path compression: when finding root, make every node on the path point directly to the root (recursive find handles this naturally)
Only increment rank when both roots had equal rank — attaching a smaller tree under a taller one doesn't change height
exercise.test_code:
uf = UnionFind(5)
assert uf.find(0) != uf.find(1), '0 and 1 start separate'
uf.union(0,1)
assert uf.find(0) == uf.find(1), 'After union(0,1) same root'
uf.union(1,2)
assert uf.find(0) == uf.find(2), 'Transitivity: 0 and 2 share root'
assert uf.find(3) != uf.find(0), '3 still isolated'
assert uf.union(0,2) == False, 'union of already-connected nodes returns False'
uf.union(3,4)
assert uf.find(3) == uf.find(4), 'After union(3,4) same root'
print('PASS')
